commit.message:
fix: create reusable setup-go action to fix Go module cache in CI workflows (#8257)

Most CI workflows were using `actions/setup-go` without
`cache-dependency-path`, causing the action to scan for all `**/go.sum`
files in the repo. This produces an inconsistent cache key, leading to
frequent cache misses and modules being re-downloaded on every run.

## Changes

- Created a reusable composite action
`.github/actions/setup-go/action.yml` that wraps `actions/setup-go` and
always passes both `./go.sum` and `./internal/tools/go.sum` as
`cache-dependency-path`:

```yaml
- uses: actions/setup-go@...
  with:
    go-version: ${{ inputs.go-version }}
    cache-dependency-path: |
      ./go.sum
      ./internal/tools/go.sum
```

- Updated all 21 CI workflow files to use `./.github/actions/setup-go`
instead of calling `actions/setup-go` directly

The `check-go-version.sh` lint script continues to work correctly since
workflow files retain `go-version: 1.26.x` in their `with:` sections.
The `setup-go-tip` action is intentionally excluded — that workflow
explicitly purges the module cache immediately after setup.

commit.message:
Use gotestsum for all test execution (#8256)

Replaces the raw `go test | tee` pipeline and `bash -c "... | COLORIZE"`
patterns with [`gotestsum`](https://github.com/gotestyourself/gotestsum)
across all test targets for consistent formatting, automatic failure
reruns, and cleaner output.

## Changes

- **`internal/tools/tools.go`** — pins `gotest.tools/gotestsum v1.13.0`
as a tracked tool dependency
- **`scripts/makefiles/Tools.mk`** — adds `GOTESTSUM` variable; installs
it via `install-test-tools`
- **`Makefile`**:
- Adds `GOTESTSUM_FLAGS=--format pkgname-and-test-fails --format-icons
hivis` shared across targets
- `test`: replaces `bash -c "... go test -v ... | COLORIZE"` with
`gotestsum`
- `cover`: replaces `bash -c "... go test ... | tee"` with `gotestsum
--rerun-fails --packages ./...`
  - Removes `GOTEST`, `GOTEST_QUIET`, `COLORIZE` variables
- **`scripts/makefiles/IntegrationTests.mk`** — migrates
`all-in-one-integration-test`, `jaeger-v2-storage-integration-test`,
`storage-integration-test` to `gotestsum`

## Output format

```
✅  github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/internal/metrics (2.28s)
➖  github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/internal/metrics/benchmark (1.02s)
❌  github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/internal/metrics/foo (0.8s)
    --- FAIL: TestFoo (0.00s)

DONE 37 tests in 6.1s
```

- `pkgname-and-test-fails`: one line per package; failed test output
inlined
- `hivis`: high-visibility ✅ / ➖ / ❌ icons
- `--rerun-fails`: auto-reruns failing packages (up to 2×) in `cover`
- `--packages ./...` required alongside `--rerun-fails` per gotestsum's
API

commit.message:
perf: cache RatpackConstants to avoid recomputing transcendentals per eval

RatpackConstants::new() computes pi, e, ln2, ln10 via series expansions
on every evaluate() call (~18ms in debug builds). Now cached in CalcState
keyed by (radix, precision), recomputed only when settings change.

- Add constants_cache field to CalcState with ensure/cached_constants API
- Eagerly warm cache at REPL startup (before first prompt)
- evaluate() reuses cached constants for subsequent evaluations

commit.message:
Move param type conversion util out of tracestore API into v1adapter (#8255)

`internal/storage/v2/api/tracestore/reader.go` imported
`internal/storage/v1/api/spanstore` solely to support the
`ToSpanStoreQueryParameters()` conversion helper — an undesirable v1
dependency in a v2 API package.

## Changes

- **`tracestore/reader.go`**: Remove `ToSpanStoreQueryParameters()`
method from `TraceQueryParams` and drop `spanstore`/`jptrace` imports.
The v2 tracestore API package is now free of v1 dependencies.
- **`v1adapter/tracereader.go`**: Add `GetV1QueryParameters(query
tracestore.TraceQueryParams) *spanstore.TraceQueryParameters` as an
exported function. The v1adapter package is the correct home for v1↔v2
conversion logic.
- **Tests**: Move `TestToSpanStoreQueryParameters` →
`TestGetV1QueryParameters` into `v1adapter/tracereader_test.go`.

```go
// Before (in tracestore package — wrong layer):
func (t *TraceQueryParams) ToSpanStoreQueryParameters() *spanstore.TraceQueryParameters { ... }

// After (in v1adapter package — correct layer):
func GetV1QueryParameters(query tracestore.TraceQueryParams) *spanstore.TraceQueryParameters { ... }
```

Fix look-ahead and turnover handling in backtest flows, add regression coverage, and introduce a docs index to make project guidance easier to navigate. Also ignore local Cursor metadata from git tracking.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: add one-pick daily profit simulation to signal API

Return a historical one-share top-pick backtest summary in /api/signal, including cumulative profit (JPY) and cumulative return percentage, with regression coverage for the new calculation logic.

Extend /api/signal one-pick backtest output with hit rate and win/loss day counts so users can evaluate historical accuracy alongside profit.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: add last-7-day performance breakdown to one-pick metrics

Expose a last7Days block in /api/signal one-pick metrics so users can inspect recent weekly hit rate, profit, and per-day trade details.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: add daily buy-candidates basket performance to signal API

Expose a new dailyBuyCandidatesBacktest block in /api/signal that evaluates buying all daily buy candidates (1 share each) and selling at close, including hit rate, cumulative profit, and last-7-day breakdown.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: make subspace PCA robust to dynamic universe dimensions

Infer factor-space dimensions from the returns matrix and rebuild prior space when dimensions change, preventing matrix mismatch failures during reduced-universe research runs.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: add long-short candidate basket metrics to signal API

Expose dailyLongShortCandidatesBacktest in /api/signal to track daily same-day long/short basket PnL (1 share each), hit rate, and last-7-day trade breakdown.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: add weekly strategy validation with go/stop decision

Add
pm run strategy:status to run the same multi-horizon checks (top1/top3/all-buy/long-short), write report/status JSON files, and emit a GO/STOP decision based on configurable 6-month gates.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore: tighten weekly go criteria with 3m and 6m gates

Strengthen strategy restart conditions by requiring positive short-horizon performance (3m cumulative and RR) in addition to stricter 6m RR/cumulative thresholds.

Made-with: Cursor

Execute strategy_status as part of workflow runs so new strategy changes are validated immediately; keep workflow running while clearly surfacing STOP decisions.

fix: remove artificial MAX_POW_RESULT_LOG2 guard from power operator

The 4096-bit magnitude guard rejected valid computations like
2.3335656^5667.225654489 (~10^2085) before ratpack was even called.
The underlying ratpack overflow is now fixed with precision-trimmed
exponentiation, making this guard unnecessary.

commit.message:
Phase 4 Step 6: Complete Orval migration - eliminate all direct API calls

All direct API calls (api.get, api.post, api.patch, api.delete) have been replaced with Orval-generated hooks:

**Direct API Call Replacements:**
- Contact form (/contact) → useSubmitContactForm
- Profile creation & update (/profiles, /profiles/me) → useCreateProfile, useUpdateProfile
- Discovery page spot filtering → useInfiniteSpots
- Profile tabs prefetch optimization (removed complex prefetch, relies on hook queries)

**Hook Wrapper Updates (use-api.ts):**
- Fixed imports for renamed Orval hooks (useChecklistControllerCreate, useCommentReactionsControllerGetReactionSummary, etc)
- Updated contact form hook integration
- Fixed price reports 'My' queries naming (FindMyReportsInfinite instead of FindMyPriceReportsInfinite)
- Fixed votes hook to use CreateTipVote variant
- Added usePopularArea stub for compatibility

**Mutator Enhancement:**
- Updated customInstance to accept both AxiosRequestConfig and RequestInit types for better Orval compatibility

**Result:**
✓ 0 remaining direct API calls in frontend
✓ All 4 remaining direct calls migrated to Orval hooks
✓ No more manual axios/fetch usage in UI components
✓ Consistent React Query integration via Orval

**Note:** Some generated Orval types have issues (checklist params) that require backend Swagger annotation fixes, but the migration is functionally complete.

calc_manager (90 new tests, verified locally):
- conv.rs: empty/invalid string parsing, large numbers, format roundtrips
- rational.rs: u64::MAX, zero, negate, abs, dup independence, traits
- arithmetic.rs: identity laws, zero/division edge cases, GCD, comparisons
- logic.rs: shift boundaries, AND/OR/XOR properties, mod/rem edge cases
- exp.rs: domain errors (log(0), log(neg)), factorial bounds, pow edge cases

rscalc parser/eval (CI-verified, cannot build locally due to dlltool):
- lexer.rs: empty/whitespace input, invalid chars, prefix-only tokens, long strings
- parser/mod.rs: trailing ops, unclosed parens, empty parens, mode-specific ops
- eval/mod.rs: unknown identifiers, arithmetic identities, order of operations
